title: Revoke logic MBO
description:
So some background first:
At first we were hesitant to allow agents to be able to revoke a global limit request. That is because it is actually not a decline. Revoked means that we just end the lifecycle of the request without any follow-up effects.
So when you revoke a global limit request you are essentially taking it away from any detection for the system to act on it. Revoked is used mainly for when we close an account which in turn closes the account in Lugas (then lugas is responsible for handling what ever limit the customer have nationally)

if the request was in an approved state before revoking and the approved limit was, for example 2000 €, the revoking action will NOT change the limit for the user (user still has 2000 € limit). Meaning that no limit will be effected. When using revoked on a global limit request you are not doing anything to the actual limit. It is the request you are acting on only. when the system is trying to raise the limit it (the system) never looks at the requests (the requests are not the master of the actual current limit) to determine the current limit. The system looks at the lugasuser entity that we have in our regulatory-service.
The global limit requests are just a tool for users to be able to raise limits. When a request (that contains a limit)  has been approved, that approved limit is what is found in our entry for that lugas user in regulatory-service.
